title: How To Diagnose Pressure Necrosis After Rhino
filename: How To Diagnose Pressure Necrosis After Rhino.en.vtt
excerpt: Whereas with pressure on the nose tip it it's bright pink at the tip and then kind of fades laterally apart from the redness how is this presenting differently to other vios so i think because it's a pressure saw you get a much more diffuse boundary to it and because obviously th…

segments.text: whereas with pressure on the nose tip it it's bright pink at the tip and then kind of fades laterally apart from the redness how is this presenting differently to other vios so i think because it's a pressure saw you get a much more diffuse boundary to it and because obviously the pressure is diffused so it starts as a high pressure point where you put the most product and then it will fade as you go more laterally and it doesn't follow the path of major vessels so you'll often see with necrotic injury with noses you know tracks up the super trochlear artery and you can get the forehead affected that's clearly an intravascular injection whereas with pressure on the nose tip it it's bright pink at the tip and then kind of fades laterally so that's one of the things and i've also seen this in the in a chin uh which also felt quite taut so i'm wondering whether this is actually something that happens in other places too never seen it in a cheek or a lip um but those areas where there's tightness and noses are particularly that way particularly if there's just a capillary blood supply in that area i think it's
